review_text: Great spot for a good quiet short holiday. The beach fishing is phenomenal , many species of fish to caught especially from the 13th track to the beach, but make sure you bring plenty insect repellent spray as the mozzie and the horse flies can be out of control. Caravan park has great facilities and was very quiet.

Such friendly service, we asked for availability pretty much last minute, the owners were so kind and accommodating.
Lovely camp area with trees and views.
Bathrooms are clean and tidy with running hot water.
The only downside was the play park area could be a little bigger and accomodating to all ages.
Easter is a busy time and we had a great experience. Thanks again.

Loch Sport Caravan Park is one of those relaxed coastal parks that reminds you why people love coming to places like Gippsland in the first place.

The park sits right in the middle of Loch Sport between Lake Victoria and the famous Ninety Mile Beach, so you’re perfectly positioned whether you’re into boating, fishing, beach walks, or just enjoying the quiet coastal lifestyle.

What really makes the place though are the owners Simon and Kylie. They’re friendly, approachable and clearly care about the park and their guests. It has that welcoming small-town feel that’s getting harder to find these days.

The park itself is well kept and peaceful. It’s an easy walk to the lake foreshore and only a couple minutes drive to Ninety Mile Beach.

I’m a Google Local Guide, and during our visit I actually created and published a Google Street View tour of the park so future visitors can explore the layout before arriving. Hopefully that helps people get a feel for the place ahead of time.

Loch Sport itself is a bit of a hidden gem — abundant wildlife, beautiful lake views, endless beaches, and a laid-back atmosphere that’s perfect if you want to slow down and enjoy nature.

If you’re travelling through the Gippsland Lakes region, Loch Sport Caravan Park is definitely worth stopping at.
Great park. Very clean. Helpful staff. We will be back.
Nice clean park, new owners went out of their way to make our stay nice. Great location for anyone into water sports.

To say I am grateful for the customer service and kind-hearted support I experienced from Simon and Kylie is a massive understatement!

A no-fuss late check in after the reception was closed- had hiked from Seaspray 4 days prior, no water left and a knee injury.
Friendly chats and enjoyed relaxing in Loch Sport so much that we decided to stay for a 2nd night.

An extreme wind picked up and the tent decided to go walkabout. Corralling our belongings we bunkered in the kitchen area.  Simon popped in to check on us and gave us a free upgrade to a lovley caravan annex.

Since the power was off for the afternoon due to the crazy winds, Kylie dropped be to offer us some potatoes and foil to cook in the fire. Although we had a gas stove this was greatly appreciated.

They have gone above and beyond any expectations I define as hospitality and I wish Simon and Kylie and their team the very best with their future.

Much gratitude and kind regards-

-Keith

Very quiet when we visited which meant that it was a great place to stay for 3 nights. Quite a lot of permanent vans but mostly empty. Seemed like there was some effort just starting to upgrade the campground and facilities. A few mozzies around (early December) but would guess that there would be more at nearby bush camps.

Lovely park. Extremely clean. Very nice staff.
The only downside were the mossies.
Excellent facilities and friendly staff.
Boys fishing weekend.. sleep in tent quite weekend great ..like it

We spent a few nights at Loch Sport Caravan Park back in November and couldn't wait to get back for an extended stay. We just spent 9 nights in a powered site and we loved every minute of it. Our kids were close to the playground and a place to swim was just a few metres away. The camp ground is beautiful and I loved walking around it with my 2 pups who were welcomed to the park.

The amenities were pristine and we would like to personally thank Leonie for keeping everything so clean and neat. We also thank Belinda and Ray for being so welcoming and nothing was too much of a bother.

We were sad to leave our holiday and have already had discussions about making it an Easter tradition at the lovely park. But I have no doubt we'll return well before then as it is quickly becoming a favourite spot for our family.

Thanks Loch Sport Caravan Park. Until we meet again! Salmela and Briggs families.

It was a nice little caravan park with the surf near it but way to many bill ants, mozzies and flies also the play equipment for kids is extremely limited my nephew and daughter didn't even touch the play ground and just played soccer on the grassy area they said it was dangerous. My husband told the management about the flies and stuff but they said "What do you expect we are in the bush" not even trying assist us thank you hope things could get better with this caravan park.
Beautiful, peaceful park and one of our favourite places. Owners could not be more friendly or helpful. Have visited here many times and love the people and the area.

Worst experience ever. Turning up with a 6yo and a 1yo with our always on leash mini foxy. We set up camp and almost immediately heard swearing and loud bad language, this continues as the afternoon goes on. Our children were approached by drunk strange adults a few times trying to show them things.. As the night went on we became more concerned about their behaviour. Their dog got loose and rushed our mini foxy almost grabbing it. When we told the drunken person who came after it, "it should be on a leash mate" we were verbally  assaulted and threatened. Packed up our site at 10pm with no where to go swarmed with mosquitoes. We reported this to the management on our way out only to be informed that these were permanent residents of the park... Be careful with your children near the camp-site..

A old fashioned caravan park in a beautiful bush setting located close to the water where you fish. It is also close to the RSL and you can get a bus shuttle to the local hotel- there is also a walking track into town with lots of near by animals. The caravan site we were given was a sand and grass patch with lots of room to set up. With a little bit of  patience we got  the caravan onto a level section.The amenities were clean and tidy and the staff were very attentive

Just had a great three days here with a few old mates. Lovely park in a beautiful coastal bush setting.  Facilities were all very clean, camp kitchen was very useful and the location next to the lake is just perfect. If you like a spot surrounded by beautiful birdlife and kangaroos, you couldn’t ask for more.

Fuss free stay, nice and quiet while we were here. The mozzies have started (spring time) but easily managed with repellents and no one suffering so far. Clean amenities, cheap coin laundry. Happy days.

Outdated..
Not worth the 60.00 per night...
Mosquitoes were relentless in September...
One of my absolute favourites when taking the bike out for some camping. Great spot near the beach, and the facilities are super clean.
Thank you for supporting us at Our Haven Wildlife Shelter Inc you guys are truly AMAZING
Stayed in caravan for a week. Huge sites, really friendly operators... Out of peak times, it was really quiet, which suited us.

Review for loch sport Caravan Park

Stayed here for 4 nights from Monday
Had few problems with the march flys as we had Bushman’s with us
Tuesday afternoon we had thunderstorms and the Power Went out For a long time due to The storms  we had a thunder box in the car so I can keep my phone charged as well as mums as well

Had telstra Service

No longer as of 8 pm yesterday no Telstra phone service
All of Victoria is with out Power also No phone service

Showers are good

Have a Esim on my device

Phone service Restored
But down again at 4.55 am

Power has been restored Wednesday morning

Very happy 😃
Having a great time

Will come again
Thxs Belinda and Ray for great time here
And to Steve for sorting our Jocky Wheel

Will come again soon
Belinda was great and very helpful, amenities were clean and camping site big. Location lakeside was convenient and plenty of activities in the area. Recommended

Fantastic place.  Water views and cute little beaches on walk tracks.
Great service and we camped and had some boating to do nearby, lovely bushy gardens.  Enjoyed to the service very much, excellent.
The wildlife is very pleasant to listen to throughout the day very relaxing area.
Great van park. Excellent amenities and service. Loved it

Great caravan park right on lake Victoria with a jetty close by . Some nice shops locally.90 mile beach only ten minutes from caravan park. Toilet/shower blocks well maintained and grounds are kept clean. Beautiful park. Alot of mozzies.
Big clean park, great with our dogs 🐕

Lovely spot for a night away. Walking distance to the general store and pub.
Facilities clean, great camp kitchen.
Fisherman's paradise.
Just spent 2 nights there.  Friendy people. Sooo Quiet.  Clean Facilities.  Great night with Camp fire. Relaxing.
